---
title: "Nuttall's Meadowbeauty (Rhexia nuttallii)"
slug: "rhexia-nuttallii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354768490/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Nuttall's Meadowbeauty"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Myrtales"
tax_family: "Melastomataceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Myrtales", "Melastomataceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354768490/medium.jpeg" alt="Nuttall's Meadowbeauty" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/dmfraser">
dmfraser</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/201019380">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Myrtales
-   **Family:** Melastomataceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=127911)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A herbaceous wildflower characterized by its square stems, narrow leaves, and showy pink to purple flowers with prominent yellow stamens. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, wet prairies, and roadside ditches. It typically blooms in summer and is visited by bees and other pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/127911-Rhexia-nuttallii)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2688)
